results.question: What caps work with the 30ml Boston Round?
results.response:
The 30ml Boston Round has a 20-400 neck finish, and it's compatible with a few closure types.

For simple caps, there's a black lid in the 20-400 thread. For roll-on caps, you have six options — tall profile, available in shiny gold, matte gold, shiny silver, matte silver, shiny black, and matte black. And if you need a dropper closure, those come in black or white bulb with plain, shiny gold, or shiny silver collar finishes.

Everything is guaranteed to fit since it all threads onto the same 20-400 neck. Are you pairing a closure with a specific formula type, or is the finish and colour the main consideration for you?

results.question: I need a dropper for my 9ml Cylinder. What fits?
results.response:
Good news — I have clarity on both variants. Here's the honest picture:

The 9ml Cylinder comes in two neck sizes, and neither currently has a dropper listed as a compatible component. The 13-415 neck version (the taller sprayer-style bottle) is compatible with sprayers, caps, and roll-on caps. The 17-415 neck version (the roller ball style) supports sprayers, roll-on caps, and lotion pumps.

Before I suggest a workaround, it would help to know a bit more about your formula — what are you filling the bottle with, and what's the viscosity like? That'll tell me whether a different vessel in a similar size might serve you better, or whether one of the existing applicator options could actually work just as well for your needs.

results.question: Do you have lotion pumps that fit a 100ml Cylinder?
results.response:
Yes — there are seven lotion pumps that fit the 100ml Cylinder perfectly, all with the matching 18-415 thread. They come in matte gold, shiny gold, shiny black, matte silver, matte silver with a clear overcap, shiny silver, and matte copper. All are guaranteed to seat correctly since they're part of our integrated system.

What finish direction are you leaning toward for your product line?

results.question: I have a thick perfume oil, like honey consistency. What roll-on bottles do you recommend?
results.response:
I want to make sure you get the best result here — a roll-on applicator relies on the ball spinning freely in its socket, and with a honey-consistency oil, the viscosity is typically too high for that mechanism to work well. The ball won't roll smoothly, and you'll likely get frustrating under-dispensing or no flow at all.

For a thick perfume oil, I'd actually steer you toward two better options:

A dropper bottle gives you precise, controlled application — perfect for a concentrated oil where a little goes a long way. Or, if your oil is in the attar tradition, a tola bottle is the classic choice — the traditional dabbing vessel used for exactly this style of fragrance. It's elegant, functional, and tells a beautiful story about the product.

Which direction resonates more with you — the precision of a dropper, or the ritual of a tola-style dabber?

results.question: I'm making an eau de parfum. Should I use roll-on bottles?
results.response:
Great question — and an important one to get right before ordering.

Eau de parfum is alcohol-based, which means roll-on applicators aren't the right fit. The roller ball is engineered for oil-viscosity liquids — thin fragrance oils, serums, beard oils. Alcohol-based formulas like EDP will seep past the ball and leak, which is the last thing you want after labelling and boxing your product.

For an EDP, a fine mist sprayer is the classic and most effective choice. It delivers a soft, diffuse spray that's ideal for alcohol-based fine fragrance — exactly the kind of experience your customers expect when they pick up a perfume bottle.

Are you thinking of a particular bottle style or size for the line?

results.question: I'm packaging a thick body lotion. Would a dropper bottle work?
results.response:
A dropper isn't the right fit for a thick lotion — the viscosity is too high for a dropper tube to dispense cleanly, and it would be frustrating for your customers to use.

For thick emulsions like body lotion, a lotion pump is the right applicator — it's engineered for that viscosity and delivers a controlled, satisfying dose every time.

Would you like me to pull up our lotion pump bottle options, or do you have a particular size or style in mind?

results.question: Tell me about your Boston Round collection.

The Boston Round is a timeless workhorse of the fragrance and wellness world — that classic, rounded-shoulder silhouette you'll recognise from apothecary shelves and essential oil collections. We carry it in three sizes: 15ml, 30ml, and 60ml, across three glass colours — clear, amber, and cobalt blue.

Amber is particularly popular for essential oils and light-sensitive formulations since it filters UV naturally. The collection pairs with droppers and roll-on applicators (both metal and plastic roller ball), and the neck finishes run 18-400 and 20-400 depending on the size.

results.question: Show me a 30ml spray bottle.
results.response:
We have a lovely selection of 30ml spray bottles. At that size, two silhouettes stand out right away:

The Slim — a slender, refined clear glass bottle with a vintage-style bulb sprayer in lavender with a shiny silver collar. Clean lines, very tactile in hand.

The Sleek — also 30ml in clear glass, available with either a lavender bulb sprayer and silver collar, or a matte silver sprayer with a matte silver collar for a more understated, monochromatic look.

And the Diva — a curvier, more sculptural 30ml in clear glass, dressed with a lavender bulb sprayer and silver collar. It has a lot of presence on a vanity.

All three feature the antique bulb sprayer — that classic squeeze-bulb atomiser with a beautiful, collectible feel. If you were looking for a standard pump sprayer instead, I can pull those up as well.

results.question: Do you manufacture your own glass?
results.response:
We don't manufacture glass — Best Bottles is a sourcing partner and curated supplier. We work with a global network of artisan glassmakers to bring premium vessels to our collection, and every piece is selected to meet our quality standards for the beauty and fragrance industry.

What makes us distinctive is that we use these same bottles for Nemat's own fragrance lines, sold at Sephora, Ulta, and Whole Foods — so every vessel in our catalog has been validated in real retail environments before it reaches your brand.

Are you exploring packaging for a specific product or line?

results.question: Why should I choose Best Bottles over other suppliers?
results.response:
There are a few things that genuinely set us apart, and I'll share them honestly rather than as a sales pitch.

We use these exact bottles for Nemat International's own fragrance lines — sold at Sephora, Ulta, and Whole Foods. That means every vessel in our collection has been validated in a real retail environment, not just listed in a catalog. When you choose a bottle from us, you're choosing something that has already earned shelf space at major retailers.

The second thing is our system guarantee. Every bottle, closure, sprayer, dropper, and cap we sell is engineered to work together. The number-one pain point in this industry is sourcing a beautiful bottle and then discovering the cap doesn't seat properly, or the sprayer threads don't match. We eliminate that entirely. Our components are cross-referenced and guaranteed to fit — no guesswork, no costly surprises before a launch.

Third is where we sit in the market. Most suppliers serve either the hobbyist end — small minimums, inconsistent quality — or the enterprise end, where you need to commit to tens of thousands of units before anyone takes your call. We occupy the space between: professional-grade packaging, small-batch flexibility, and the kind of consultative guidance that helps a growing brand make the right decision the first time. There are no unit minimums, just a fifty-dollar order floor, so you can test before you commit.

And finally, we're a family-owned Bay Area company with over two decades in the fragrance industry. You're not working with a call center — you're working with people who understand what it takes to bring a fragrance product to market.

What kind of product are you developing? I'd love to point you toward the right starting place.

results.question: I'm launching a luxury perfume line. Should I go with Elegant or Diva bottles?
results.response:
These two families each tell a very different story on shelf, and the right choice really depends on the aesthetic your brand is building. Here's how I'd frame it:

The Elegant is the more versatile of the two — a clean, elongated silhouette that reads as timeless and apothecary-inspired. It comes in four sizes (15ml through 100ml), clear or frosted glass, and supports the widest range of applicators including fine mist sprayers, vintage bulb sprayers with tassel, droppers, and roll-ons. If your line spans multiple formats or price points, the Elegant gives you room to grow within a cohesive visual language.

The Diva is a bolder, more sculptural statement — it comes in 30ml, 46ml, and 100ml, with the same clear or frosted glass options, but a more curvaceous, fashion-forward profile. It's a strong choice if your brand identity leans toward femininity and drama. One thing worth noting: the Diva carries a consistent 18-415 neck finish across all sizes, which simplifies sourcing closures across your range.

For a luxury perfume line, both pair beautifully with fine mist sprayers or vintage bulb sprayers — the two most elevated applicator presentations.

The question I'd ask you: is your brand's visual identity more quiet and refined, or does it have a more expressive, couture personality?

results.question: I need bottles for my essential oil business. Where do I start?
results.response:
Essential oils are a wonderful category to work in — the packaging decisions you make here will directly affect product integrity, customer experience, and shelf life.

A few quick questions to point you in the right direction:

Are your oils light-sensitive? Most essential oils benefit from amber glass, which blocks UV degradation. And how do you want your customers to apply the oil — a dropper for precise dispensing, a roll-on for direct skin application, or an open pour with a reducer?

results.question: What does 18-415 mean? I see it on your product pages.
results.response:
Great question — it's a standard industry notation for a bottle's neck finish.

The first number, 18, is the outer diameter of the bottle's neck opening in millimetres. The second number, 415, refers to the thread pattern — specifically the number of turns and the pitch of the threading that a closure screws onto.

So an 18-415 neck needs a closure — cap, sprayer, dropper, or roller — that is also labelled 18-415. That match is what guarantees a proper, leak-free seal.

Is there a specific bottle or closure you're trying to pair up?
